reviews.summary: The manuscript proposes a method called SVEA for stabilizing deep Q-learning with ConvNets and Vision Transformers under data augmentation. The authors identify causes of instability when using data augmentation in common off-policy RL algorithms and propose a simple yet effective technique for stabilizing this class of algorithms. They perform extensive empirical evaluation on various benchmarks and show that their method greatly improves stability and sample efficiency of ConvNets under augmentation and achieves generalization results competitive with state-of-the-art methods.
reviews.main_review: The manuscript addresses a significant problem in reinforcement learning (RL) - generalizing learned skills to novel environments. The authors identify two main causes of instability when applying data augmentation to common off-policy RL algorithms: (i) indiscriminate application of data augmentation resulting in high-variance Q-targets; and (ii) Q-value estimation strictly from augmented data resulting in over-regularization. They propose SVEA, a framework that consists of three components: (1) only applying augmentation in Q-value estimation of the current state, without augmenting Q-targets used for bootstrapping; (2) formulating a modified Q-objective that optimizes Q-value estimation jointly over both augmented and unaugmented copies of the observations; and (3) optimizing the actor strictly on unaugmented data. The manuscript is well-organized, and the writing is clear. The authors provide extensive empirical evaluation on various benchmarks, including DeepMind Control Suite and robotic manipulation tasks, and show that their method greatly improves stability and sample efficiency of ConvNets under augmentation and achieves generalization results competitive with state-of-the-art methods. The method also scales to RL with Vision Transformers (ViT) architectures. However, the authors could provide more analysis on the limitations of their method and the potential negative societal impact.
